away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

injuries.name: Sami Whitcomb
injuries.comment: This is a tough blow for Whitcomb and the Mercury as she will now miss the first month or so of the WNBA season. Whitcomb served as a part-time starter during her first season in Phoenix last year, averaging 9.1 points, 2.6 rebounds and 2.5 assists in 23.9 minutes per game across 43 regular-season appearances (20 starts). Kiana Williams could see an uptick in minutes with this news.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

injuries.name: Taylor Thierry
injuries.comment: Thierry will remain on the inactive list to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental contract. Her next chance to play will come in Minnesota on Saturday.

injuries.name: Taina Mair
injuries.comment: Mair will sit out a sixth consecutive contest as the Storm manage her availability while signed to a developmental contract. The rookie guard's next chance to play will come Saturday in Minnesota.

injuries.name: Dominique Malonga
injuries.comment: Malonga will miss an eighth consecutive contest while in the league's concussion protocol, and she remains without a definitive timetable for a return. The second-year center's next chance to play will come Saturday in Minnesota. With Malonga out, Awa Fam, Mackenzie Holmes and Jade Melbourne are candidates for increased playing time.

injuries.name: Ezi Magbegor
injuries.comment: Magbegor sustained her injury while playing for Australia in the 2026 FIBA Basketball World Cup Qualifiers, and she appears to be on track to miss the start of the WNBA regular season as a result. The 26-year-old has played a prominent role in Seattle's frontcourt across the past several seasons, most recently averaging 8.0 points, 6.2 rebounds, 2.2 blocks and 2.1 assists in 27.3 minutes per game while starting every regular-season matchup in 2025, while her absence to begin the 2026 season could lead to increased opportunities for Dominique Malonga, Stefanie Dolson and rookie Awa Fam.
